title: How to Use SIM-Based Calling in GoHighLevel — Call Leads Instantly
description: Learn how to use SIM-based calling in GoHighLevel to dial leads instantly from your mobile app. Step-by-step setup, call logging, and agency best practices.
html_content:
You're sitting at your desk, a hot lead comes through, and you're stuck copying their phone number, opening your phone's dialer, pasting it in, and hitting call. By the time you connect, they've already moved on to a competitor.

This workflow kills conversion rates—and it's completely unnecessary.

SIM-based calling in GoHighLevel eliminates friction. With a single tap, you can launch your phone's native dialer directly from the GoHighLevel mobile app, with the lead's number already pre-filled. No business phone setup. No extra tools. Just instant dialing from the device you already carry.

For digital marketing agencies managing dozens of clients and hundreds of leads, this feature is a game-changer. In this guide, I'll walk you through exactly how to use it, why it matters, and how to set up manual call logging so your team stays organized.

Ready to speed up your lead response? Start your free 30-day trial of GoHighLevel (double the standard trial) and test SIM-based calling with your own leads.

📋 In This Guide

  • What Is SIM-Based Calling in GoHighLevel?
  • How SIM-Based Calling Works
  • Key Benefits for Agencies and Sales Teams
  • Prerequisites and Limitations
  • Where You Can Make SIM-Based Calls

  • Best Practices for Agency Teams

What Is SIM-Based Calling in GoHighLevel?

SIM-based calling is a native dialing feature built directly into the GoHighLevel mobile app. Instead of forcing you to use a business phone number or VoIP system, it leverages the phone line already on your device—your personal SIM card and existing mobile plan.

When you tap the call icon next to a contact in GoHighLevel, the app opens your phone's native dialer with the lead's number automatically pre-filled. You then tap to call using your standard cellular connection, just like dialing from your contacts app.

The result? Zero setup friction. Zero extra fees. Zero copy-paste delays.

This is particularly powerful for solo agents, small teams, and agencies that don't want to maintain a separate business phone infrastructure while building out their lead calling operation.

💡 Pro Tip

SIM-based calling works best when paired with manual call logging. After your conversation, you'll log the outcome in GoHighLevel so your CRM stays accurate and your team has a complete call history.

How SIM-Based Calling Works

The mechanics are straightforward:

  1. Navigate to a contact, opportunity, or conversation in the GoHighLevel mobile app where a phone number is listed.
  2. Tap the phone icon next to the contact's name or number.
  3. Your device's native dialer launches with the number pre-populated in the dial field.
  4. Confirm and tap call to connect using your SIM-based mobile line.
  5. Log the call manually after your conversation by returning to GoHighLevel and recording the outcome, notes, and duration.

The entire flow takes seconds. There's no waiting for a business phone system to initialize, no switching between apps, and no risk of dialing the wrong number because it's auto-filled.

The call itself goes through your standard cellular network, so call quality depends on your mobile coverage—just like any other phone call you make. If you have good signal, you'll have crystal-clear conversations.

Key Benefits for Agencies and Sales Teams

1. Faster Lead Response Times
Speed is everything in sales. The difference between calling a lead within 30 seconds versus 3 minutes can mean the difference between a booked demo and a lost opportunity. SIM-based calling removes the friction entirely.

2. No Extra Setup or Monthly Costs
You don't need to purchase a business phone number, integrate a VoIP provider, or pay additional monthly fees for a calling system. Your existing mobile plan handles all the calls.

3. Simplified Mobile Workflow
Your team uses one app (GoHighLevel) to manage leads, view conversations, and make calls. No context-switching between multiple tools means fewer mistakes and faster execution.

4. Professional Call History Inside Your CRM
By logging calls manually in GoHighLevel, you create an audit trail of every conversation. When a team member hands off a lead, the next rep has full context.

5. Ideal for Agencies Managing Multiple Client Campaigns
Agencies often juggle calls across 10+ client campaigns in a single day. Having instant dialing at your fingertips keeps you responsive without needing separate phone systems per client.

Prerequisites and Limitations

Prerequisites:

  • Active GoHighLevel account with access to the mobile app (iOS or Android)
  • Mobile device with an active cellular plan and SIM card
  • Phone number(s) stored in GoHighLevel contacts
  • Mobile app version that supports SIM-based calling (current versions do)

Limitations to Know:

  • Outbound calls only: SIM-based calling initiates outbound calls; it does not handle inbound call routing to your GoHighLevel number.
  • Manual logging required: Unlike a full business phone system, call duration and outcomes aren't automatically logged. Your team must manually record this data after each call.
  • Dependent on cellular coverage: Call quality relies on your personal mobile network's signal strength, not a dedicated VoIP infrastructure.
  • Caller ID shows personal number: Leads will see your personal phone number on their caller ID, not a business number. For agencies, this may require clear caller ID management or branded dialing setup through a separate system if client branding is critical.
  • No call recording built-in: GoHighLevel's SIM-based calling doesn't include native call recording. Agencies requiring call recordings should use their phone's built-in recording feature or a dedicated call recording app (with proper legal consent).

Where You Can Make SIM-Based Calls

SIM-based calling works from three primary locations within the GoHighLevel mobile app:

1. Contacts Section
Navigate to any saved contact and tap the phone icon. The contact's primary phone number launches in your dialer.

2. Opportunities Section
When viewing an opportunity (a sales deal or lead stage), tap the phone icon to call the associated contact directly from that opportunity record.

3. Conversations Section
If you're texting or messaging a lead through GoHighLevel, you can instantly switch to a call by tapping the phone icon in that conversation thread.

This multi-location access is intentional—it lets your team call leads from whatever view they're already in, without hunting for contact details.

Setting Up Manual Call Logging

Since SIM-based calling doesn't auto-log call data, your team needs a consistent manual logging process. Here's the recommended workflow:

Step 1: Make the Call
Tap the phone icon in GoHighLevel, connect with the lead, and have your conversation.

Step 2: Return to the Contact Record
Immediately after hanging up, return to GoHighLevel and navigate back to that contact's record or the related opportunity.

Step 3: Log Call Details
In the Notes or Activity section, record:

  • Call date and time
  • Call duration (approximate is fine)
  • Outcome (interested, not interested, callback scheduled, voicemail left, etc.)
  • Key points discussed or next steps

Step 4: Update Opportunity Stage if Needed
If the call result warrants moving the lead to a different stage in your pipeline, update that now so your CRM reflects current status.

💡 Pro Tip

Create a standardized call logging template for your team (e.g., "[Date] Called – [Outcome] – [Next Step]"). Consistency makes it easy to scan call history and ensure no leads fall through the cracks.

Best Practices for Agency Teams

1. Assign Call Windows by Team Member
If multiple people are calling the same lead pool, assign specific calling hours to avoid duplicate calls. Document this in your internal SOP.

2. Use Status Updates to Track Call Progress
Create custom opportunity stages like "Called – Waiting for Response," "Called – Scheduled Callback," or "Called – Not Interested." This keeps your pipeline visual and organized.

3. Build a Call Script in Your CRM Notes
Save key talking points and objection responses in the contact or opportunity record so agents can quickly review before dialing.

4. Train on Immediate Logging
The biggest failure point is forgetting to log calls. Make it a team habit: call ends → return to GoHighLevel → log outcome. No exceptions.

5. Consider Pairing SIM-Based Calling with a Business Phone System
For agencies managing high-volume calling or requiring branded caller IDs, use SIM-based calling for quick outreach and layer in a VoIP number (like GoHighLevel's business calling) for more formal client-facing calls.

6. Monitor Response Rates
Track which team members convert calls fastest and which scripts generate the most callbacks. Use this data to coach underperformers and replicate success.

7. Set Expectations for Personal Number Visibility
If you're calling on behalf of a client, brief them that leads will see your personal number. This is normal for agency work, but transparency prevents confusion.
